evidence_excerpt: She was discharged 1 week after hospitalization; at last follow-up, she was doing well with no sequelae 13 months after discharge.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: No sequelae reported 13 months after discharge; neurologic studies normal during recovery.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: Before the plasma exchange, she began to regain consciousness.
She became alert after plasma exchange and returned to full consciousness on the second hospital day.
Plasma exchange was performed to remove unknown toxins.
Subsequent hemodialysis was performed after plasma exchange.
At last follow-up, she was doing well with no sequelae 13 months after discharge.

notes_on_uncertainty: The report describes three family members. This summary focuses on the 2-year-old sister, who has documented long-term follow-up. Timing is normalized to her hospital admission, as the exact moment of definitive diagnosis was not explicitly stated.
